profiles: atomicpi-desktop  (11000 MB, XFCE — build after headless proven)

hardware_notes.audio: XMOS xCORE USB audio → TI TAS5719 class-D amp → powered outputs. GPIO 349 deasserted at boot by atomicpi-hold-xmos.service.

v1_milestone.name: Atomicty_Pi v0.1
v1_milestone.description: First functional build meeting the documented baseline
criteria: Debian Trixie, Armbian uefi-x86, current kernel
ext4 root, systemd-networkd minimal + NetworkManager server images

stages.name: armbian-base-build
stages.description: compile.sh builds uefi-x86 Trixie base image

stages.name: customize-image
stages.description: overlay rsync + service enable + DKMS modules

stages.name: flash-and-test
stages.description: Flash to eMMC, run firstboot.sh, work through test-matrix.md

pivot_history.to: Armbian uefi-x86 + userpatches overlay (single config+customize pair)
pivot_history.reason: Armbian already maintains uefi-x86/Trixie at Standard tier.

pivot_history.to: multi-profile configs + overlay/ tree + extensions/ hook
pivot_history.reason: Cleaner separation: static files in overlay, package injection in extension hooks, runtime steps only in customize-image.sh. Multiple named profiles (minimal/server/dev/desktop). No board file or Armbian fork needed.

notes.msg: XMOS card name in asound.conf is 'XMOS'. Verify with 'aplay -l' on first live boot.
sysfs GPIO (/sys/class/gpio/) is deprecated since ~5.3 but present in 6.x. If Armbian current-branch kernel removes it, switch services to 'gpioset' from the gpiod package.
customize-image.sh git-clones DKMS modules during build — requires network in chroot. Normal for Armbian builds. If offline, pre-clone into armbian/userpatches/packages/ and update path.
Armbian kernel headers package naming for uefi-x86 should be linux-headers-current-x86. Verify after first build with 'dpkg -l | grep linux-headers' in the image.
SSH hardened in customize-image.sh: PasswordAuthentication no. Users must set a key via preset-firstrun.sh or interactively on first boot. Adjust if passwordless SSH is needed for a lab image.
BIOS default boot order after CMOS reset may prioritize PXE over eMMC. Document exact behavior in boot-uefi.md after first hardware test.
